meta_title: For Founders: freelance pitching to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Freelance Pitching to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your SaaS. Learn how to use freelance pitching to fund your MVP, utilizing Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster than the competition.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of market feedback and capital. By shifting from 'building in a vacuum' to freelance pitching, you secure non-dilutive funding while validating your product. Use Antigravity to structure your pitch and Claude Code to rapidly prototype the features your clients actually pay for.
content.intro:
Your desktop is a graveyard of half-baked repositories. You started with a vision, but the 'feature creep' monster ate your momentum, and now you’re staring at a codebase that doesn't generate a single cent. The silence from the market is deafening, and your motivation is hemorrhaging with every commit that doesn't ship.

But here is the brutal truth: you aren't stuck because of technical debt; you're stuck because you aren't being paid to build. Freelance pitching forces you to solve real problems for real people, turning your development process into a revenue-generating engine. We are going to weaponize Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your stalled ideas into sellable assets.

steps.title: Identify the High-Value Pain Point
steps.description: Stop building features you think are cool. Use a framework to extract the specific business problem that a client will pay to solve immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project repository and extract the top 3 high-value features that solve a painful business problem for a small business owner. Structure this as a value-proposition pitch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze repo and extract high-value pain points'

steps.title: Structure the Sales Asset
steps.description: Create a lean, persuasive pitch deck or landing page that focuses on the transformation, not the technical implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a concise, high-converting pitch script for a SaaS MVP. Focus on ROI, time-saved, and the specific problem identified in step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'create landing_page.md with persuasive copy based on identified pain points'

steps.title: Rapid Prototype with Claude Code
steps.description: Build the 'minimum viable' version of the pitch-winning feature. Don't touch the rest of the codebase; focus strictly on the delivery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Outline the minimal architecture required to build the feature identified in step 1. Keep it serverless and fast to deploy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude build 'implement core feature logic with test coverage'

steps.title: Automate Outreach Pipelines
steps.description: Use your technical skills to scrape potential leads and automate the initial contact, ensuring your pitch reaches decision-makers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Python script to identify potential clients on LinkedIn/Twitter based on the niche identified in step 1 and generate personalized outreach messages.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'create outreach_bot.py using selenium and openai api'

steps.title: Iterate Based on Client Feedback
steps.description: Once you get a 'yes', use the payment to fund further development. Use the actual client usage data to inform your next build cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feedback loop log template that captures user friction points from the initial prototype deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup feedback_loop.json and logging middleware'

pro_tips: Never build a feature until a client has signed a Letter of Intent or deposited funds.
Use Claude Code to write tests before the code; it prevents the 'feature creep' that kills side projects.
Pitch the 'Outcome', not the 'Tool'. Clients don't care that you use AI; they care that you save them 10 hours a week.
Keep your MVP under 500 lines of code. If it's larger, you're building a platform, not a solution.
Treat every pitch as a technical requirement document; if you can't explain it simply, you don't understand the business value.
content.next_steps: Once you secure your first paying client, use the revenue to outsource the maintenance of your MVP and pivot to your next high-ticket vertical.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your code rot on your hard drive while the market waits for a solution. Use this framework to transform your technical skills into a sustainable, revenue-generating SaaS business today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
